perturbation.original_text: A consumer who purchases a specially Designated Sponsor Product (as defined below) will receive exactly one (1) unique pin code (a "Pin Code") in-pack. Such a consumer wil l be entitled to visit a custom interactive landing page at the CharityTunes.com website to enter the unique code and consumer's valid email address to receive downloads up to a maximum of either three (3), five (5), or seven (7) free MP3 song downloads having a retail value of $1.29 per song or less.
perturbation.changed_text: A consumer who purchases a Designated Sponsor Product will receive a maximum of one (1) unique pin code (a "Pin Code") in-pack. A consumer may be entitled to visit a custom interactive landing page at the CharityTunes.com website to enter the unique code and consumer's valid email address to receive downloads up to a maximum of either three (3), five (5), or seven (7) free MP3 song downloads having a retail value of $1.29 per song or less.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'exactly one' to 'a maximum of one' and changing 'will' to 'may be', introduces ambiguity regarding whether every Designated Sponsor Product will necessarily contain a Pin Code. This contradicts later sections that assume each product has a Pin Code, creating uncertainty around the promotional mechanic.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A consumer who receives a Pin Code for a designated number of music downloads in accordance with paragraph 3 above will be directed to visit the custom interactive landing page in order to redeem his/her free music downloads. Consumer will have to enter the Pin Code in order to complete the transaction. Consumer will be required to enter the Pin Code exactly as it appears in the email and follow the instructions on the web site to download the selected music download. In no case may any Pin Code be redeemed later than March 31, 2010. To assist as required with the redemption process, consumers shall have access to Charity Tunes customer support by emailing at support@charitytunes.com.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity ('a maximum of one' and 'may be') regarding the provision of a Pin Code with each Designated Sponsor Product. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which assumes that a consumer 'receives a Pin Code' and 'will be directed' to a landing page to redeem it. The original text made it clear that it was 'exactly one', and consumers 'will' get a code, whereas the changed text brings uncertainty, creating a strong in-text contradiction and thus impacting the enforceability and understanding of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Designated Sponsor Products with Pin Codes may be distributed from approximately October 1, 2009 to approximately December 31, 2009 (the "Promotion Period"). Eligible Pin Codes may be entered on the www.charitytunes.com website and corresponding music downloaded from October 1, 2009 to March 31, 2010. Pin Codes entered for redemption after 11:59:59 pm PT on March 31, 2010 may be rejected as ineligible. The contents of this section are to be reflected in the Sponsor's rules for the Promotion.
perturbation.changed_text: Designated Sponsor Products with Pin Codes will be distributed from October 1, 2009 to December 31, 2009 (the "Promotion Period"). Eligible Pin Codes may be entered on the www.charitytunes.com website and corresponding music downloaded from October 1, 2009 to March 31, 2010. Pin Codes entered for redemption after March 31, 2010 will be rejected as ineligible. The contents of this section are to be reflected in the Sponsor's rules for the Promotion.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word approximately from both ends of the sentences defining the promotion period, creates a strict promotion period that is in conflict with the sentence after stating 'Eligible Pin Codes may be entered'. This create an ambiguity as to whether codes may be entered if the strict period mentioned before have been passed, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROMOTION PERIOD: Designated Sponsor Products with Pin Codes will be distributed from October 1, 2009 to December 31, 2009 (the "Promotion Period"). Eligible Pin Codes may be entered on the www.charitytunes.com website and corresponding music downloaded from October 1, 2009 to March 31, 2010. Pin Codes entered for redemption after March 31, 2010 will be rejected as ineligible. The contents of this section are to be reflected in the Sponsor's rules for the Promotion.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'approximately' to define the Promotion Period, allowing for some flexibility. The changed text removes 'approximately,' creating a strict Promotion Period (October 1, 2009 to December 31, 2009). However, the subsequent sentence states that 'Eligible Pin Codes may be entered on the www.charitytunes.com website and corresponding music downloaded from October 1, 2009 to March 31, 2010.' This creates a contradiction. The first sentence defines a specific Promotion Period that ends on December 31, 2009 for distribution of products with pin codes, while the second sentence allows pin codes to be entered until March 31, 2010, creating ambiguity about whether pin codes from products distributed after December 31, 2009, but before October 1, 2009, are valid. This inconsistency constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, since there is no clear definition when the product can be distributed.
